hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 contacts, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding off-target-conserved electrostatic interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The hypothesis is updated to emphasize that selectivity is not just a function of SSTR2 binding strength but also the ability to avoid conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are critical to achieving this.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. These positions are known to contribute to off-target binding.
Introducing charged or hydrophilic residues at these positions can disrupt conserved hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, reducing off-target binding.
